prompt_used: Create original premium horror artwork for a commercial alternate reality investigation game. Subject: investigation board. Story world: Signal From Kharon-9. Premise: Telemetry returns from a decommissioned orbital array and predicts player replies.. Setting: Mountain observatory and subterranean receiver array. Threat presence: The Quiet Orbit. Recurring motifs: black stars, phase drift, echoing pings. Puzzle visual for "Tri-band Drift Lock". Objective: Align three phase drifts shared across players to stabilize decryption.. Build layered clue board where each shard can be solved collaboratively.. Subgenre: cosmic horror. Tone: INTENSE. Immediate scene brief: Signal From Kharon-9 - Tri-band Drift Lock Puzzle Board. Grounded cinematic horror, photoreal detail, rich atmosphere, practical lighting, believable materials, analog grain, subtle lens bloom, visible depth. No title treatment, no UI overlay, no case notes panel, no labels, no watermark, no logo, no poster typography, no debug text. Show a physical evidence-board or clue collage with photographs, string, maps, stained paper, and marked surfaces. Prioritize tactile realism over readable text. Any writing should be minimal, blurred, or partially obscured.
source_prompt: Story: Signal From Kharon-9 (signal-from-kharon-9) | Hook: Telemetry returns from a decommissioned orbital array and predicts player replies. | Subgenre: cosmic horror | Tone: INTENSE | Location: Mountain observatory and subterranean receiver array | Villain: The Quiet Orbit | Villain worldview: Human choices are measurable noise in a larger equation. | Motifs: black stars, phase drift, echoing pings. Puzzle visual for "Tri-band Drift Lock". Objective: Align three phase drifts shared across players to stabilize decryption.. Build layered clue board where each shard can be solved collaboratively.
